title: schedonorus asper subsp. serotinus
intro: Schedonorus asper subsp. serotinus, also known as the late bent or serotinous meadow grass, is a type of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce swelling, and alleviate pain. The health benefits of Schedonorus asper subsp. serotinus include its ability to promote digestive health, reduce inflammation, and support immune function. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
